claim: Comment Copilot Coding (Triple C) is an effective technique where you write descriptive comments and let Aider generate implementation, enabling single-file component development across multiple frameworks
actionable: Write detailed comments describing the UI structure, state, and behavior you want, then use Aider with a consistent code marker (e.g., 'code:') to generate implementation
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Aider can write implementation in Vue, Svelte, and React with minimal framework knowledge from the user, learning syntax passively through watching generated code
actionable: Use Aider to implement features in unfamiliar frameworks; write clear requirements and learn the syntax organically from Aider's generated code patterns
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Aider sometimes duplicates script blocks or deletes existing code when making edits—a known limitation that requires manual cleanup or control-Z and redo
actionable: When using Aider, stay ready to undo and retry changes if it duplicates template blocks or removes code unintentionally; review generated code before committing
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Reactivity issues (like array.push not triggering re-renders in Svelte) require explicit prompts to Aider to fix—the agent won't always catch these without direct guidance
actionable: When state updates don't trigger UI changes in reactive frameworks, explicitly tell Aider the issue (e.g., 'prompt.push not updating the view, please fix reactivity')
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: You can scale the Comment Copilot Coding technique across multiple parallel Aider instances by firing the same prompt to each worktree, allowing simultaneous development in different frameworks
actionable: Set up multiple Aider instances in parallel terminals pointing to different worktrees/frameworks and use the same comment-based prompts across all of them
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Aider's built-in context management automatically includes open files and remembers changes within a session, allowing iterative API integration without restating context
actionable: Keep files open in Aider that you're actively working on; it will track them automatically and include relevant context in subsequent prompts
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Building front-end UIs with Aider and GPT-4 Turbo is faster than traditional manual coding for prototypes and data analytics dashboards
actionable: Use Aider with GPT-4 Turbo for rapid prototyping of data-heavy UIs; set up comment-based specifications and let the agent iterate on your descriptions
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)

claim: Using smaller insets of prompt engineering in repetitive patterns (like the same API integration prompt across multiple frameworks) is likely to become standard in future AI coding workflows
actionable: Identify repetitive coding patterns in your workflow and create reusable prompt templates that can be applied consistently across multiple implementations
source_title: Copilot Prompt Engineering: 3 UI Frameworks, 2 AI Agents, 1 Coding Assistant (AIDER CCC)
